name: Capture inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio at scale

parameters.formTitle: Capture inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio at scale

parameters.content:
### New Contact Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Dealstage). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Remove Duplicate Contacts

### Remove Duplicate Contacts

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `contactId`).

Next: Enrich — Primary Provider

### Enrich — Primary Provider

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Primary Hit?

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Primary Hit?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.company || $json.name }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Merge Enrichment Paths · ②→ Enrich — Fallback Provider

### Enrich — Fallback Provider

GET https://fallback-enrich.example.com/v1/person — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Merge Enrichment Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Merge Enrichment Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Score Enriched Records

### Score Enriched Records

Custom JavaScript: Score each contact 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Upsert into HubSpot

### Upsert into HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Next: Hot Lead?

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Hot Lead?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.score >= 80 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Next: Ping Owner Immediately

### Ping Owner Immediately

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

# Capture inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio at scale

**Pattern:** Lead Capture & Routing · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Capture inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio at scale. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual follow-ups is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Contact Form Submission → Remove Duplicate Contacts → Enrich — Primary Provider → Primary Hit? → Merge Enrichment Paths → Score Enriched Records → Upsert into HubSpot → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to lead capture & routing without manual effort
- Anyone automating follow-ups in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Scales without extra headcount
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; HubSpot private-app token; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The fallback enrichment provider only fires when the primary genuinely misses (checked explicitly, not assumed) — paying for two enrichment calls per lead when the first one already worked would double the cost for no benefit. Scoring runs after enrichment, not before, so the score reflects the richest data available rather than just what arrived on the original request.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Force the primary provider to miss (e.g. a bad URL) and confirm the fallback picks up the slack.
2. Add a third-provider rung to the waterfall after the fallback.
3. Change the hot-lead threshold and watch how often the immediate-ping branch fires.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your follow-ups process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
